name: Circuit Model
summary: The standard gate-model view of quantum computation in which qubits move through a sequence of gates and measurements.
whenToUseIt: Use it whenever an algorithm, protocol, or compilation workflow is being expressed as executable gate logic.
steps: Choose the number of qubits and initial state.
Lay out the required single- and multi-qubit gates.
Track the role of entangling operations and depth.
Decide where measurement occurs and what is classically conditioned.
Estimate how hardware noise or compilation changes the intended circuit.
example: A Bell-pair preparation or Grover iteration is naturally described in the circuit model because the ordering of gates matters to the interference pattern.
